views.markdown:
# GameUnmute

A jailbreak tweak that stops the silent switch (and Control Center silent mode)
from muting game audio.

## How it works

iOS only mutes apps whose `AVAudioSession` category is `Ambient` or
`SoloAmbient` when the device is silenced. Games almost always use one of those,
which is why their audio cuts out with the silent switch. GameUnmute hooks
`-[AVAudioSession setCategory:...]` and remaps those two categories to
`Playback`, the category that is designed to keep playing while silenced.

The hook runs in every UIKit app, but it only *changes* anything for apps that
chose `Ambient`/`SoloAmbient` — exactly the apps the silent switch would mute.
Apps using `Playback`/`PlayAndRecord` are left alone. (An earlier version gated
on `LSApplicationCategoryType == games`, but most games never set that key, so
they slipped through — hence the broader approach.)

## Notes / tuning

- **Want to limit it to one app?** Change the filter in `GameUnmute.plist` from
  `com.apple.UIKit` to that app's bundle ID.
- `Playback` is exclusive by default, so it will pause background music. If you
  want game audio to mix with your music, add `AVAudioSessionCategoryOptionMixWithOthers`
  to the options in the hooks.

## Building

Builds on GitHub Actions (macOS + Theos) — push to `main` and grab the `.deb`
from the workflow artifacts. Rootless arm64; install with Sileo/Zebra and respring.

### v1.0.0

Stops the silent switch / Control Center silent mode from muting game audio.
